sections.subtitle: Match Overview and Team Standings
sections.text: Sevilla, currently positioned at 15th in the league with 38 points, host Las Palmas, who are languishing in 19th place with 32 points. This matchup at the Ramón Sánchez Pizjuán in Seville presents an opportunity for Sevilla to capitalize on their home advantage. Las Palmas, struggling near the bottom of the table, will be desperate for points to climb out of the relegation zone. The stakes are high for both sides in this critical La Liga encounter. Historically, Sevilla have dominated this fixture with 4 wins in the last 5 matchups against Las Palmas, alongside 1 draw and no wins for the visitors.

sections.subtitle: Recent Form and Streaks
sections.text: Sevilla are enduring a tough run, having not secured a win in their last 8 matches. They have also failed to keep a clean sheet in these 8 games, often conceding first in 4 of their last 5 matches. Las Palmas, on the other hand, are on a streak of 3 consecutive losses and have not kept a clean sheet in their last 3 away games, frequently conceding first in 8 of their last 9 matches. Both teams have shown vulnerability defensively, with Sevilla seeing both teams score in 5 of their last 7 home games. This suggests an open game could be on the cards at the Ramón Sánchez Pizjuán.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Sevilla
sections.text: Sevilla’s attacking threat will likely come through Dodi Lukebakio, a forward with an impressive 11 goals this season against an expected goals tally of 9.18, overperforming by 1.82. Lukebakio also ranks 3rd in the league for successful dribbles per game at 2.4, making him a constant danger on the wing. Saúl Ñíguez, a midfielder with a strong historical record against Las Palmas, averages a 7.54 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matchups, contributing 1 goal and 2 assists. Additionally, Juanlu Sánchez adds balance to the midfield with 4 goals and 4 assists this season, showcasing his versatility.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Las Palmas
sections.text: Las Palmas will rely on Sandro Ramírez, a midfielder who has netted 9 goals against an expected goals figure of 3.93, significantly overperforming by 5.07. His contribution of 3 assists further highlights his importance to the team’s attack. Defender Álex Muñoz, who was the player of the match in their last encounter against Sevilla with a 7.7 rating, has scored 3 goals this season and will be crucial at the back. Meanwhile, José Campaña stands out with an 81% aerial duel win rate, ranking 2nd in the league, and could be key in set-piece situations.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistical Edges
sections.text: Sevilla hold a statistical edge in several areas, ranking 1st in the league for interceptions per game at 9.49 and 3rd for aerial duels won at 54.87%. Their ability to win possession in the final third, ranking 6th with 3.91 possessions per game, could put Las Palmas under pressure. However, Sevilla’s conversion rate of big chances stands at a low 30.77%, ranking 19th in the league, indicating struggles in finishing. Las Palmas, while defensively frail, have a player like Juanma Herzog with a 70.8% ground duel win rate, ranking 5th in the league, which could help disrupt Sevilla’s rhythm.

sections.subtitle: Goal Distribution and Timing
sections.text: Sevilla tend to score heavily in the latter stages of matches, with 22.45% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, totaling 10 goals in this period. They’ve also netted 8 goals between the 31st and 45th minutes, accounting for 20.41% of their tally. Las Palmas mirror this late-game potency, with 21.05% of their goals (13 in total) scored between the 76th and 90th minutes. Additionally, they’ve scored 9 goals between the 61st and 75th minutes, representing 22.81% of their total, suggesting both teams may push hard in the closing stages.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History and Trends
sections.text: In head-to-head encounters, Sevilla have an impeccable record, remaining unbeaten in their last 9 matches against Las Palmas. They’ve been the first to score in 7 of their last 7 meetings, often setting the tone early. Las Palmas have failed to keep a clean sheet in all 9 of these games, highlighting a persistent defensive issue against Sevilla. Matches between these sides have often been low-scoring, with 5 of the last 7 producing under 2.5 goals. Their most recent clash on August 16, 2024, ended in a 2-2 draw, showing Las Palmas can occasionally challenge Sevilla’s dominance.

sections.subtitle: Referee and Discipline Expectations
sections.text: The match will be officiated by Juan Martinez Munuera, who averages 4.8 yellow cards per game and 0.21 red cards per game, indicating a likelihood of cautions. Sevilla have accumulated 88 yellow cards this season, averaging 2.51 per game, while their recent home games have seen over 4.5 cards in 6 of the last 8 matches. Las Palmas, conversely, have had fewer disciplinary issues on the road, with under 4.5 cards in 6 of their last 7 away games. Expect a potentially feisty encounter given Sevilla’s tendency to commit fouls, ranking 8th in the league with 451 fouls.

sections.subtitle: Set-Piece and Corner Analysis
sections.text: Sevilla average 5 corners per game, ranking 8th in the league, but their matches have consistently had under 10.5 corners in the last 5 home games. Las Palmas also tend to see low corner counts, with under 10.5 corners in 6 of their last 8 away matches. Both teams struggle with headers, with Sevilla ranking 16th in headers scored with just 3 goals from such plays. This suggests set-pieces might not be a decisive factor in this game, and the total corner count could remain relatively low.
